title: Histamine-HisCl1 system for C elegans
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cgdpb","text":"We describe a chemical-genetic approach for inducible silencing of Caenorhabditis elegans neurons in intact animals, using the histamine-gated chloride channel HisCl1 from Drosophila and exogenous histamine. Administering histamine to freely moving C. elegans that express HisCl1 transgenes in neurons leads to rapid and potent inhibition of neural activity within minutes. C. elegans does not use histamine as an endogenous neurotransmitter, and exogenous histamine has little apparent effect on wild-type C. elegans behavior. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":67,"length":23},{"style":"italic","offset":172,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":249,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":374,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":507,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"67lhp","text":"Gloves are recommended while making or handling histamine-containing liquids. Avoid contact with eyes and skin. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
